title: Decadent Spanish Octopus Salad
description: A cultured, gooey Andalusian-inspired salad featuring octopus, potatoes, and katsuobushi, served in a bento-box.
instructions: Put octopus in a large pot, cover with water, bring to a gentle boil, then reduce to a simmer and cook for 30 minutes until tender.
While octopus cooks, prepare a pot of salted water for the potatoes, bring to a boil, and cook diced potatoes for 10 minutes until just tender, then drain and let cool.
In a large skillet, heat olive oil over high heat to generate wok-heat, then add diced red bell pepper and cook for 2 minutes until slightly charred.
Add sliced red onion to the skillet and sauté for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Transfer the sautéed vegetables to a mixing bowl and toss with cooled potatoes, olives, and parsley.
Whisk olive oil, sherry vinegar, salt, and black pepper until emulsified, then pour over the vegetable mixture and stir gently to combine.
Add shredded octopus to the bowl, toss lightly, ensuring even coating of the dressing.
Sprinkle katsuobushi over the salad and gently mix to distribute the bonito flakes.
Season to taste with additional salt and pepper if needed, then let the salad rest for 5 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Pack the assembled salad into a bento-box, ensuring it stays chilled or at room temperature for serving.
